flows.name: lean_sorry_scan
flows.description: Scan Lean4 codebase for sorry statements
flows.command_template:
python3 -c "
import requests
import json
try:
    resp = requests.get('http://localhost:8000/query', json={'query': 'SELECT file_path, sorry_count, total_declarations FROM v_proof_status WHERE sorry_count > 0 ORDER BY sorry_count DESC'})
    result = resp.json()
    print(json.dumps(result, indent=2))
except Exception as e:
    print(json.dumps({'error': str(e)}, indent=2))
"

except Exception as e:

"

"

flows.name: env_check
gpu_check
flows.description: Check AMD GPU status and ROCm availability
flows.command_template:
python3 -c "
import subprocess
import json

result = {'gpu_available': False, 'rocm_version': None, 'gpus': []}

try:
    # Check ROCm
    rocm_output = subprocess.check_output(['rocm-smi', '--showproductname'], text=True)
    result['gpu_available'] = True
    result['gpus'] = [line.strip() for line in rocm_output.split('\\n') if line.strip()]
    
    # Check PyTorch CUDA
    import torch
    result['torch_cuda'] = torch.cuda.is_available()
    result['torch_device_count'] = torch.cuda.device_count()
    if torch.cuda.is_available():
        result['torch_device_name'] = torch.cuda.get_device_name(0)
except Exception as e:
    result['error'] = str(e)

print(json.dumps(result, indent=2))
"

flows.name: lean_db_health
flows.description: Check Lean4-Automata database health and statistics

flows.name: quick_proof_report
flows.command_template:
python3 -c "
import requests
import json

try:
    # Get proof status summary
    resp = requests.get('http://localhost:8000/query', json={
        'query': '''
            SELECT 
                COUNT(*) as total_files,
                SUM(sorry_count) as total_sorries,
                SUM(CASE WHEN sorry_count = 0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as complete_files,
                AVG(sorry_count) as avg_sorries_per_file
            FROM v_proof_status
        '''
    })
    
    result = {
        'summary': resp.json(),
        'top_sorry_files': [],
        'timestamp': __import__('datetime').datetime.now().isoformat()
    }
    
    # Get top 10 files with most sorries
    resp2 = requests.get('http://localhost:8000/query', json={
        'query': 'SELECT file_path, sorry_count FROM v_proof_status WHERE sorry_count > 0 ORDER BY sorry_count DESC LIMIT 10'
    })
    result['top_sorry_files'] = resp2.json()
    
    print(json.dumps(result, indent=2))
except Exception as e:
    print(json.dumps({'error': str(e)}, indent=2))
"

flows.name: lean_proof_audit
flows.description: Audit Lean proof map (summary by default)

report_type.description: Type of audit report: summary (counts only) or full (includes mismatches)

flows.name: lean_cohesion_ranking
flows.description: Rank modules by cohesion score (top 15 by default)

limit.description: Number of top modules to return

flows.name: lean_proof_edit_guard
flows.description: Guard Lean proof edits by classifying git diff risk and lake build diagnostics

diff_file.description: Optional unified diff file to analyze; defaults to current tracked Lean git diff

build_log_file.description: Optional lake build log file to classify

run_build.description: Run the build command and classify its output

build_command.description: Build command used when run_build is true

backup_path.description: Backup archive or directory associated with the edit

metadata.description: Flow definitions for Lean4-Automata Forum CLI harness
notes: Flows marked with 'placeholder: true' are not fully implemented
Subprocess flows use Python3 for portability
Proxy flows forward requests to local services
All flows include error handling and JSON output
